mnemonics.title: S-K-E-T-C-H Quick Recall
mnemonics.content: S - Speed (rapid iteration), K - Keep it simple, E - Explore multiple ideas, T - Think before digital, C - Communicate visually, H - Hands-on low stakes

mnemonics.title: Sketching Process Steps
mnemonics.content: T - Think (understand problem), H - Hand (start drawing), I - Iterate (repeat quickly), N - Note (add annotations), K - Kill bad ideas (discard), E - Evaluate (get feedback)

mnemonics.title: Low-Fi vs Hi-Fi
mnemonics.content:
LOW-FI: Lines + Boxes + Simple = Structure Focus
HIGH-FI: Colors + Typography + Refined = Visual Focus

acronyms.expansion: Low-Fidelity - Simple sketches focusing on structure and layout without detailed visuals
acronyms.usage: Used in early ideation stages for quick exploration

acronyms.expansion: High-Fidelity - Detailed sketches resembling final product with colors and typography
acronyms.usage: Used when design direction is established and visual refinement is needed

acronyms.expansion: User Experience - The overall experience of a user interacting with a product
acronyms.usage: Sketching helps plan the user journey and interaction flow

acronyms.expansion: User Interface - The visual elements through which users interact with a product
acronyms.usage: Sketching helps layout buttons, menus, and visual hierarchy

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a blueprint with labeled rooms - boxes represent content areas (header, sidebar, main content, footer) without decorations
Think of a subway map - arrows show paths between screens/stations, indicating how users navigate through the product
Think of newspaper headlines - larger elements catch attention first, just as bigger text boxes represent primary content
Use circle badges on clickable items - like putting stickers on interactive buttons to distinguish them from static content

analogies.analogy: Sketching is like a musician rehearsing - quick, imperfect practice before the polished performance (final design)
Low-fi sketches are like architectural floor plans - they show where walls and rooms go, not the paint colors or furniture
Sketching multiple ideas is like taking photos in burst mode - more attempts increase chances of capturing something great
Sketching is to digital design what sketching is to painting - the foundational exploration before refinement
